title: google_maps_flutter
objectives: Embed a Google Map with markers/polylines
Handle iOS + Android API keys and permissions

sections.markdown: `google_maps_flutter` embeds a native Google Map inside a Flutter widget. You provide API keys per platform, add markers/polylines/circles, and drive camera position via a `GoogleMapController`.

Native maps are the heaviest platform view Flutter embeds. Keep markers under a few hundred, cluster the rest, and avoid rebuilding the whole GoogleMap when only markers change — pass the marker set as a const-friendly Set.

Committing API keys in source. Restrict keys per platform + application (bundle ID / SHA1) in the Google Cloud Console, and inject via build config (`--dart-define`, Info.plist, AndroidManifest placeholders).

For heat maps or thousands of points, prefer a tile-based layer or clustering (fluster / google_maps_cluster_manager). Cache tiles for offline where feasible. Consider Mapbox/MapLibre if pricing/features push you.

flashcards.back: Info.plist (iOS) + AndroidManifest.xml (Android), restricted to your bundle IDs in Cloud Console.
Cluster them; don't render thousands independently.
It's a native platform view — heavier than pure Flutter widgets.

options: Restricted per platform + bundle ID and injected

challenges.text: Show a map centered on a city with one marker.
Draw a polyline between two points and fit camera bounds.
Overlay a custom tile source with offline caching.

interviewQuestions: How does the plugin embed native maps?
How do you secure API keys?
How do you handle many markers?
How do you drive the camera from state?
